concerns.text: T027 mandates executing a pilot study with '≥5 real participants' on a GitHub Actions free-tier runner. This violates the execution environment constraints defined in plan.md and spec.md (CPU-only, no manual intervention, no external human recruitment). The task silently relaxes the requirement for 'real human data' (FR-004/SC-005) to an impossible condition for the specified CI/CD environment, creating a fatal implementation gap.

T011 instructs fetching RAVDESS from 'parlance/raVDESS' on HuggingFace. FR-001 in spec.md mandates downloading from the 'official source' but leaves the URL empty. The task assumes a specific third-party mirror without verifying it against the spec's 'official source' requirement, risking a violation of FR-001 if the mirror is not the canonical source.

T013 mandates generating stimuli for 'all 8 emotion categories' as a hard requirement. The spec's Edge Cases explicitly state the system must 'log warnings and proceed with available categories' if the dataset is incomplete. T013 lacks the conditional logic to handle missing categories, effectively weakening the robustness requirement defined in the spec's edge case handling.

T025 proposes using Streamlit for the data collection app. The plan.md and spec.md define the target platform as a 'computational pipeline' on a 'GitHub Actions free-tier runner' with no manual intervention. A web-based Streamlit interface introduces a runtime dependency and hosting complexity not authorized in the plan, violating the 'no manual intervention' reproducibility constraint (Constitution Principle I) and the defined execution environment.

T023 is marked as an 'Exploratory check only' to verify correlations between flanker count and spatial frequency energy. SC-002 (Clutter Metric Validity) requires metrics to be computed for *all* stimuli and the relationship to be quantified. Treating this validation as optional exploratory work rather than a mandatory verification step leaves SC-002 partially unverified, silently relaxing the acceptance criteria.
